en.extract:
The blue-masked leafbird (Chloropsis venusta) is a species of bird in the family Chloropseidae. It is endemic to humid montane forest in the western regions of the Indonesian island of Sumatra. Generally, it lives in areas from 600 to 1,500 m (1,969–4,921 ft) in elevation. It is the smallest species of leafbird.
It is considered near threatened due to habitat loss.

The '''blue-masked leafbird''' ('''''Chloropsis venusta''''') is a [[species]] of [[bird]] in the family [[Chloropseidae]]. It is [[Endemism|endemic]] to humid [[montane forest]] in the western regions of the [[Indonesia]]n island of [[Sumatra]]. Generally, it lives in areas from 600 to 1,500 m (1,969–4,921 ft) in elevation. It is the smallest species of [[leafbird]].<ref name="IUCN">{{cite iucn |author=BirdLife International. |year=2018 |title=''Chloropsis venusta'' |volume=2018 |article-number=e.T22704969A131660711 |doi=10.2305/IUCN.UK.2018-2.RLTS.T22704969A131660711.en |access-date=26 March 2021}}</ref>

It is considered [[near threatened]] due to [[habitat loss]].
